# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with attached EBS volumes for application data

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC for the EC2 instances
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Subnet for the EC2 instances
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - t3.xlarge
      - t3.2xlarge
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
      - m5.2xlarge
      - m6i.large
      - m6i.xlarge
      - m6i.2xlarge
    Description: EC2 instance type
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Optional SSH key pair name
  AmiId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Image::Id
    Description: AMI ID for the EC2 instances
  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 2
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 10
    Description: Number of EC2 instances to launch
  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Root EBS volume size in GiB
  DataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 1
    Description: Application data EBS volume size in GiB
  DataVolumeType:
    Type: String
    Default: gp3
    AllowedValues:
      - gp3
      - gp2
      - io1
      - io2
      - st1
      - sc1
    Description: EBS volume type for application data
  SecurityGroupIngressCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR allowed to access the instances

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref SecurityGroupIngressCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  LaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !Ref AmiId
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyName
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Arn: !GetAtt InstanceProfile.Arn
        NetworkInterfaces:
          - DeviceIndex: 0
            AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
            Groups:
              - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
        BlockDeviceMappings:
          - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
              VolumeType: gp3
              DeleteOnTermination: true
              Encrypted: false
          - DeviceName: /dev/xvdf
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref DataVolumeSize
              VolumeType: !Ref DataVolumeType
              DeleteOnTermination: true
              Encrypted: false
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash -xe
            yum update -y || true
            mkdir -p /app/data
            if [ -b /dev/xvdf ]; then
              mkfs -t xfs /dev/xvdf || true
              grep -q '/dev/xvdf' /etc/fstab || echo '/dev/xvdf /app/data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2' >> /etc/fstab
              mount -a
            fi
            chown -R ec2-user:ec2-user /app/data || true

  ApplicationInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: application-instance

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: EC2 instance ID
    Value: !Ref ApplicationInstance
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security group ID
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
  InstanceProfileName:
    Description: IAM instance profile name
    Value: !Ref InstanceProfile
